title: Restore-Drill Cadence And Timer Hardening Execution
objective: Add a bounded recurring restore-drill cadence for the already-live `personal-notes` encrypted offsite lane and harden the deployed backup timer with explicit jitter, start limits, and rotated file logs, while keeping runtime, ingress, auth, and installed-app posture unchanged.
dependency_note: Packet 004 closed the basic host-native offsite backup cadence. This execution packet is bounded to the next two maintenance-hardening steps: recurring restore-drill cadence and hardened timer behavior on the deployed backup surface.
required_inputs: The existing live Backblaze-backed Restic repository configured on the Olares host
The existing machine-local env file at /home/olares/code/personal/.env.personal-offsite-backup
The installed host-native backup runner and systemd timer from packet 004
The validated restore-drill logic already present in infra/private/run-personal-notes-offsite-backup-remote.ps1
constrained_outputs: A host-native runner for recurring restore drills against the existing encrypted repository
An updated operator helper that can manage both backup and restore-drill timer profiles
Deployed backup timer hardening with explicit jitter, start limits, and rotated append-only file logs
Updated operator and architecture docs that distinguish backup cadence from restore-drill cadence
A dated handoff recording live install, status, and run-now proof for the weekly restore-drill lane
No public ingress, shared auth, Olares app registration, or installed-app promotion
validation_steps: Keep the live personal-notes runtime host-only or mesh-tunneled and do not change ingress or auth posture.
Confirm the deployed backup timer now includes RandomizedDelaySec, StartLimitIntervalSec, StartLimitBurst, and append-only file logs with logrotate retention.
Install a second system-level timer for restore drills rather than overloading the backup cadence.
Confirm the restore-drill timer is enabled and active with a real next-run timestamp.
Confirm a manual restore-drill run completes successfully in the isolated drill root and validates recovered archive integrity without touching the live Notes runtime.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes helper, operator, and bounded host automation hardening work only. It does not authorize database writes, public ingress, shared auth, or Olares app registration.
closure_note: This execution packet now includes live proof on the real Olares host. The shared schedule helper now supports `backup` and `restore-drill` profiles, the deployed backup timer includes `RandomizedDelaySec=20m`, `StartLimitIntervalSec=6h`, `StartLimitBurst=2`, and append-only file logs with weekly logrotate retention, and the new weekly restore-drill timer completed a successful `run-now` that restored snapshot `76b8155c` into `/home/olares/apex-restore-drills/personal/memos/20260502T182526Z` while validating recovered archive integrity. The live runtime boundary remains unchanged.
next_action: Maintain the encrypted offsite lane through the installed backup and restore-drill timers plus bounded reruns of status and restore-drill, and only open a later packet if wider promotion beyond the current private boundary is desired.
